summary: Building a maintenance management app with features including a maintenance fund ledger (with delete/edit entries) and a reset button for total collections and expenses. Currently implementing the reset button in 6 micro-steps to work around free plan build limitations. Draft 50 is the last known stable version used as rollback checkpoint.
architectural_notes: Builds are split into 6 micro-steps to avoid free plan tool iteration limits: (1) Reset button UI, (2) Backend reset for collections, (3) Backend reset for expenses, (4) Wire button to collections reset, (5) Wire button to expenses reset, (6) Confirmation dialog + feedback
Draft 50 is the rollback checkpoint — user can say 'revert to draft 50' to restore last stable state
known_issues: Free plan causes repeated build failures due to tool iteration limits; off-peak hours (2am-7am UTC) recommended for complex builds

existing_features.title: Add a 'Reset' button to the MaintenanceManagement component UI. The button should be visible only to admin users (consistent with the existing admin-only 'Add Maintenance Expense' button pattern). The button should be styled with a destructive/warning style (e.g., red or orange) to indicate it is a dangerous action. The button should render in the header/controls area of the maintenance management section. The button must have no click handler logic attached yet — it should be inert (or log a placeholder console message). Do not add any backend calls or state changes.

new_feature_requests.summary: Delete and edit entries on maintenance fund ledger
Reset button for maintenance management to clear total collections and total expenses
